# Copilot Instructions for helm-for-home

This is a Helm chart repository maintained by **HomeOpsHub**, publishing self-hosted application charts for home/homelab use.

---

## Repository Layout

```
charts/
  stable/   # Production-ready charts (16 charts)
  beta/     # Experimental / in-progress charts
common/     # Shared Helm helpers (if used)
docs/       # Documentation
scripts/    # Utility scripts
.github/
  workflows/  # CI/CD workflows
```

Each chart lives in its own directory and follows the standard Helm scaffold:

```
Chart.yaml
values.yaml
README.md          # auto-generated by helm-docs — do NOT edit by hand
templates/
  _helpers.tpl
  deployment.yaml
  service.yaml
  ingress.yaml
  ingressRoute.yaml  (Traefik IngressRoute, used in many charts)
  serviceaccount.yaml
  hpa.yaml
  pvc.yaml / pvc.yml
  configmap.yaml
  secret.yaml
  NOTES.txt
  tests/
```

---

## Chart Conventions

### Chart.yaml
- `apiVersion: v2`
- `type: application`
- `version:` follows **SemVer** — bump `patch` for fixes, `minor` for new features
- `appVersion:` must match the upstream container image tag (quoted string)
- Always include `keywords`, `home`, `sources`, `maintainers` (name: `HomeOpsHub`, url: `https://github.com/HomeOpsHub`), and `annotations.category`

### values.yaml
- Top-level keys: `replicaCount`, `image`, `imagePullSecrets`, `nameOverride`, `fullnameOverride`, `serviceAccount`, `podAnnotations`, `podLabels`, `podSecurityContext`, `securityContext`, `service`, `ingress`, `resources`, `autoscaling`, `persistence` (if needed), then app-specific sections
- `image.tag` defaults to `"latest"` or pinned upstream tag
- Every key must have a comment describing its purpose (for helm-docs)
- App-specific config goes in a named block (e.g. `mariadb:`, `redis:`, `env:`)

### _helpers.tpl
- Define: `<chart>.name`, `<chart>.fullname`, `<chart>.chart`, `<chart>.labels`, `<chart>.selectorLabels`, `<chart>.serviceAccountName`
- Use `trunc 63 | trimSuffix "-"` on name helpers

### NOTES.txt
- Must be rich, chart-specific, and useful — not generic boilerplate
- Show the access URL using `{{ .Values.service.port }}` and release name
- Reference key config values (credentials, ports, persistence paths)
- Include a "Next Steps" or tips section
- Never use `.Values.database.user` — check the actual `values.yaml` structure first

### IngressRoute (Traefik)
- Use `ingressRoute.yaml` (or `ingressroute.yml`) for Traefik `IngressRoute` CRD alongside the standard `ingress.yaml`
- Controlled by `ingressRoute.enabled` in values

### Persistence
- Use `pvc.yaml` (or `pvc.yml`) for PersistentVolumeClaims
- Controlled by `persistence.enabled` in values

---

## README.md — helm-docs

**READMEs are auto-generated by [helm-docs](https://github.com/norwoodj/helm-docs). Never edit them manually.**

To regenerate after changing `Chart.yaml`, `values.yaml`, or `NOTES.txt`:

```powershell
# helm-docs binary (download once)
$dest = "$env:TEMP\helm-docs"
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $dest | Out-Null
Invoke-WebRequest -Uri "https://github.com/norwoodj/helm-docs/releases/download/v1.14.2/helm-docs_1.14.2_Windows_x86_64.zip" -OutFile "$dest\helm-docs.zip"
Expand-Archive -Path "$dest\helm-docs.zip" -DestinationPath $dest -Force

# Run from repo root
& "$env:TEMP\helm-docs\helm-docs.exe" --chart-search-root charts
```

CI (`docs.yml`) will fail if READMEs are stale. Always regenerate before pushing.

---

## Workflows

| File | Trigger | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `lint.yml` | push/PR → `main` | `helm lint` all charts + chart-testing |
| `docs.yml` | push/PR → `main` | Validates READMEs are up-to-date with helm-docs |
| `security.yml` | push/PR/weekly | Trivy config scan on rendered templates; SARIF upload to Security tab |
| `release.yml` | tag push | Lint, package, and publish charts |
| `stale.yml` | schedule | Marks stale issues/PRs |
| `copilot-review.yml` | PR open/sync | Requests `copilot-pull-request-reviewer` (requires Copilot code review enabled in repo Settings) |

### Action versions in use
- `actions/checkout@v4`
- `azure/setup-helm@v4` (version `v3.17.0`)
- `helm/chart-testing-action@v2.7.0`
- `helm/kind-action@v1.12.0`
- `github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v4`
- Trivy: installed via `curl -sfL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/aquasecurity/trivy/main/contrib/install.sh | sh -s -- -b /usr/local/bin` (do **not** use `aquasecurity/trivy-action` — its setup sub-action is broken)

### Workflow rules
- All workflows must have `concurrency.cancel-in-progress: true`
- All jobs must have `timeout-minutes`
- Least-privilege `permissions` block on every job
- `continue-on-error: true` on optional/informational steps (e.g. SARIF upload)
- Guard SARIF upload with `hashFiles('trivy-results.sarif') != ''`

---

## Stable Charts

| Chart | Category | Notable Config |
|---|---|---|
| `adguard-home` | Networking | DNS, ad-blocking; needs PVC |
| `cloudbeaver` | Database | DB admin UI; configmap + secret |
| `custom-error-pages` | Networking | Traefik error pages |
| `freshrss` | RSS | MariaDB via `mariadb.*` keys (not `database.*`) |
| `glances` | Monitoring | System monitoring |
| `gotify` | Notifications | Push notification server |
| `it-tools` | Utilities | Developer toolbox |
| `netdata` | Monitoring | Real-time metrics |
| `open-wearables` | Health | Wearable data sync |
| `outline` | Knowledge Base | Wiki; needs Redis + DB |
| `plex` | Media | Plex Media Server |
| `spotify` | Media | Spotify integration |
| `teslamate` | IoT | Tesla data logger; needs Postgres |
| `uptime-kuma` | Monitoring | Uptime monitoring |
| `vitasync` | Health | Health data sync |
| `whoogle` | Privacy | Private Google search proxy |

---

## Key Rules

1. **Never edit `README.md` files by hand** — always use helm-docs
2. **Always verify `.Values.*` paths against the actual `values.yaml`** before referencing them in templates or NOTES.txt — nil pointer errors fail `helm lint`
3. **Bump `version` in `Chart.yaml`** whenever any chart file changes (triggers CI re-release)
4. **Do not use `aquasecurity/trivy-action`** — use the `install.sh` script + direct `trivy` CLI instead
5. **Copilot review workflow** requires "Copilot code review" to be enabled in GitHub Settings → Copilot → Code review; the step uses `continue-on-error: true` to avoid blocking PRs if not yet enabled
6. **Branch protection** for `main`: apply rules manually in GitHub Settings → Branches (cannot be done via workflow)
7. When adding a new chart: follow the stable chart scaffold, add all standard templates, write a rich `NOTES.txt`, run helm-docs, bump version, open a PR
